macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: U.S. Treasury yields surge to highest levels since 2007 as inflation worries hit markets
items.summary: Treasury yields jumped to multi-year highs, with the 30-year yield reaching 5.19%, as investors fretted that inflation may stay sticky and pressure the Federal Reserve to keep policy tighter for longer. The move weighed on stocks and reinforced a broader risk-off tone across global markets.

items.title: Wall Street slips as higher yields and inflation concerns dampen sentiment
items.summary: U.S. stocks fell as rising bond yields added pressure to valuations and renewed inflation concerns offset optimism in other parts of the market. The decline was broad enough to keep investors focused on interest-rate expectations rather than near-term earnings support.

items.title: Mortgage rates climb to 6.56% as borrowing costs rise with Treasury yields
items.summary: The Mortgage Bankers Association said average U.S. mortgage rates rose to 6.56%, reflecting the move higher in Treasury yields. Higher financing costs are adding fresh headwinds to the housing market and consumer affordability.

items.title: Fed official says more rate hikes cannot be ruled out
items.summary: A Federal Reserve official reiterated support for the current policy stance but said additional rate increases remain possible if inflation does not cool sufficiently. The comments reinforced expectations that the Fed remains alert to upside inflation risks.

items.title: Asian equities tumble as surging U.S. bond yields and geopolitical uncertainty weigh on risk assets
items.summary: Markets across Asia sold off amid the jump in U.S. yields and renewed uncertainty related to U.S.-Iran tensions. The move highlighted how U.S. rate and macro developments continue to drive global equity sentiment.

items.title: Dollar holds near six-week highs on rate-hike bets
items.summary: The U.S. dollar remained firm near six-week highs as traders priced in the possibility of higher-for-longer interest rates. The stronger dollar added pressure on other major currencies and emerging-market assets.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto market rebounds as Bitcoin, Ethereum and XRP rise
items.summary: Major cryptocurrencies moved higher over the past day, with Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP and Dogecoin all gaining after President Trump’s remarks on Iran helped ease geopolitical fears. The broader market followed the move, reflecting improved risk sentiment across digital assets.

items.title: Crypto market cap jumps about 2% as Bitcoin holds near $77,000
items.summary: The global crypto market cap rose roughly 2% in 24 hours to around $2.7 trillion, with Bitcoin, Ethereum and XRP leading gains. Despite the bounce, Bitcoin remained near $77,000 while ETF and spot demand trends were still mixed.

items.title: Bitcoin stays steady near $77,000 as bond selloff and weak spot demand weigh on markets
items.summary: A market update noted Bitcoin consolidating around $77,000 even as Treasury yields rose and spot demand softened. The report also highlighted sustained ETH ETF outflows and continued underperformance in some major altcoins.

items.title: Crypto commentators highlight potential policy catalysts and institutional ETH buying
items.summary: A live market discussion pointed to possible U.S. policy developments around crypto legislation and government Bitcoin buying as bullish catalysts. The stream also emphasized continued accumulation of Ethereum by BitMine and ongoing strength in Solana-related metrics.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Xbox hires analyst Matthew Ball as chief strategy officer
items.summary: Microsoft brought in Matthew Ball, an influential gaming analyst and blogger, to help steer Xbox’s strategy as it prepares for the next phase of its gaming business. The move suggests a renewed focus on long-term planning amid broader changes at the division.

items.title: Quantic Dream shuts down Spellcasters Chronicles
items.summary: Quantic Dream is ending development on its MOBA Spellcasters Chronicles after the game struggled to gain traction against genre leaders like League of Legends and Dota 2. The cancellation adds to a growing list of live-service projects that failed to find an audience.

items.title: PlayStation State of Play set for June 2 with Wolverine spotlight
items.summary: Sony announced an hour-long State of Play for June 2 featuring new game updates and reveals from top studios. The presentation will include a closer look at Marvel’s Wolverine, which is expected to be one of the biggest first-party showings.

items.title: Fortnite returns to the App Store globally
items.summary: Epic Games’ Fortnite is reportedly coming back to the App Store worldwide, expanding beyond its U.S. comeback. The move marks another major milestone in the long-running dispute between Epic and Apple over mobile app store policies.

items.title: Xbox Game Pass adds late-May lineup including major releases
items.summary: Microsoft’s late-May Game Pass wave includes new additions such as Forza Horizon 6 and Remnant, along with the day-one release of Luna Abyss. The lineup underscores Game Pass’s continued emphasis on high-profile first-party and launch-day content.

items.title: Sony raises PS Plus prices for one- and three-month plans
items.summary: Sony is increasing prices for shorter PlayStation Plus subscription tiers, citing ongoing market conditions. The change could affect players who rely on month-to-month or quarterly membership options.

items.title: Sony hit with class action lawsuit over tariff-related price hikes
items.summary: Sony is facing a class action lawsuit alleging it profited from tariff-related increases in hardware prices. The case is similar to recent legal actions involving other major consumer tech and gaming companies.

items.title: California lawmakers move to preserve access to online games
items.summary: State lawmakers in California are advancing legislation aimed at protecting access to online games after shutdowns. The campaign was informed by game preservation advocates, reflecting growing concern over the fate of live-service titles.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
